behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is algorithmic quality of the heuristic vs an optimal solver on in-memory coordinates/distances. No OS/network/subprocess required. A mock is not the risk; the risk is incorrect heuristic logic or distance accumulation.
behaviors.test_description: Generate many deterministic stop sets (n<=20), compute exact optimum (e.g., Held-Karp/branch-and-bound in-process), run heuristic, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case. Include edge geometries (clustered, collinear, outlier) to catch worst-case regressions.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is module wiring: per-leg distance + road-type speed lookup + time-window accumulator. A pure function test could miss wrong config mapping or wrong leg-to-road-type association.
behaviors.test_description: Run estimator through the service layer with real config loading and distance-leg inputs (no external API). Use a multi-leg route with mixed road types and assert per-leg and cumulative ETA windows match expected math, including rounding/units.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Boundary is deterministic arithmetic/comparison logic on total route time vs remaining shift time plus excess calculation. No external dependency needed.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test validator with table-driven cases: below limit, exactly equal, just over, and large overrun. Assert rejection boolean and exact excess duration value/units.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: To catch real failures, test must exercise real HTTP client behavior and response parsing for the geocoder 'no results' payload. In-process mocks can hide serialization/status/body-shape bugs. Requires real HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start a test HTTP geocoding server (or wire to a sandbox endpoint) that returns valid coordinates for most addresses and a real 'no results' response for one. Call the full service endpoint/process, assert skipped-address report contains that address, and assert optimizer output excludes it while still returning a valid route for remaining stops.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Risk is cross-module behavior: normalization/dedup + item merge + optimizer input construction. Unit tests of dedup alone can miss duplicate reintroduction downstream.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke route-planning service path with repeated address entries carrying different item payloads. Assert output has one stop for that address, merged items are preserved, and route sequence includes that stop exactly once.
